About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to produce green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy.
Read more on our website
www.ceres.tech
or follow us on
LinkedIn
.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 12:00
RNS Number : 7522U
Ceres Power Holdings plc
27 February 2026
CWR.L
27 February 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres Power" or "the Company")
Total Voting Rights and admission of further securities to trading
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology,
confirms that as at 27 February 2026, the Company had 194,795,875 Ordinary Shares in issue, with one voting right each.
The Company does not hold any shares in treasury. The number of Ordinary Shares with voting rights is therefore 194,795,875.
Accordingly, these figures may be used by shareholders as a denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or change to their interest in, the Company, under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
In compliance with the Financial Conduct Authority's PRM Admission to Trading on a Regulated Market rules, the Company confirms that 83,679 shares allotted pursuant to the Company's share plans were admitted to trading over the calendar month to date. Information required in compliance with PRM 1.6.5 is detailed below:
Issuer name:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI):
213800N7AAHSXQUOA733
Regulated market on which the transferable securities have been admitted to trading:
Main Market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange
Name, type and ISIN of the shares:
Ordinary shares
GB00BG5KQW09
Number of further transferable securities admitted to trading covered by the notification:
83,679
Total number of transferable securities admitted to trading (taking into account the further issuance):
194,795,875
Confirmation that the further transferable securities admitted to trading are fungible with transferable securities already admitted to trading:
Confirmed.
Date range of securities admitted covered by the notification
1 February 2026 to 26 February 2026.
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Dominic Murray (Company Secretary)
Tel: +44 (0)7526 196529

Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy.

latest.title: Notice of Results
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0112U
Ceres Power Holdings plc
24 February 2026
CWR.L
24 February 2026
Ceres Power Holdings plc
("Ceres", the "Company")
Notice of Results
Investor presentation via Investor Meet Company
Horsham, UK:
Ceres Power Holdings plc (CWR.L), a leading developer of clean energy technology, will announce its results for the year ended 31 December 2025 on Thursday, 26 March 2026.
Management will be hosting a live presentation relating to full year results via Investor Meet Company at 09:30 GMT.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted ahead of the event via the Investor Meet Company dashboard, or at any time during the live presentation. Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and register to attend via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/ceres-power-holdings-plc/register-investor
Ends
For further information visit
www.ceres.tech
or contact:
Ceres Power Holdings plc
Merryl Black
Tel: +44(0)7770 853463
Email:
investors@cerespower.com
About Ceres
Ceres is a leading developer of clean energy technology: fuel cells for power generation and electrolysers to green hydrogen. Its asset-light, licensing model has seen it establish partnerships with some of the world's largest companies, such as Doosan, Delta, Denso, Shell, Weichai, and Thermax. Ceres' solid oxide technology supports greater electrification of our energy systems, including AI data centres, commercial and industrial applications, and produces green hydrogen at high efficiencies as a route to decarbonise emissions-intensive industries such as ammonia, steelmaking, and electrofuels. Ceres is listed on the London Stock Exchange ("LSE") (LSE: CWR) and is classified by the LSE Green Economy Mark, which recognises listed companies that derive more than 50% of their activity from the green economy. company_30s.what_they_do: CWR Ltd. supplies specialized equipment for the renewable energy sector, focusing on wind turbine control systems and grid‑integration hardware. Their products help utilities manage variable power output and keep the grid stable.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Renewable energy is expanding worldwide, and grid‑integration tech is a critical bottleneck. CWR’s systems are used by several major wind farms, giving them a foothold in a market that is expected to grow rapidly as governments push for decarbonisation.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a lean operating phase, with cash burn higher than revenue. Recent filings show a modest uptick in orders but no breakthrough product launch yet. The share price is trading far below its 52‑week highs, indicating a steep decline from previous valuations.

rns_narrative: The latest notice of results reveals a small but positive earnings surprise, suggesting cost controls are starting to pay off. The holding‑statement update shows a modest increase in institutional ownership, hinting that some investors are beginning to see value. The block‑listing interim review signals that the company is engaging with regulators to address compliance issues, which could clear a major hurdle.

These three threads—slight earnings upside, incremental institutional interest, and proactive regulatory engagement—paint a picture of a company that is still struggling but making incremental adjustments to stabilize its business.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the company’s wind‑farm contracts and the potential upside if the new compliance review clears. They see the current price as a bargain for a tech that is still in demand.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Skeptics worry that the company’s cash burn remains high and that the regulatory review may uncover deeper issues. They fear the company could be forced to cut back on R&D or even face a forced sale of assets.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past week the chatter has shifted from neutral to mildly bullish after the earnings surprise, but overall engagement remains low, leaving the market largely indifferent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there’s no public buzz, which means a surprise catalyst could catch the market off‑guard.

near_term_catalysts: A quarterly earnings release that confirms revenue growth above 5% year‑on‑year, indicating the cost‑control measures are working.
Completion of the block‑listing interim review with a favorable outcome, removing any regulatory red‑flags.
A volume spike that exceeds the 50‑day average by 30% on a day when the price touches the 20‑day moving‑average support level, showing renewed buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in institutional shares (e.g., a 10% increase in a single day) and a clear statement from the board that the compliance review is over. Any announcement of new wind‑farm contracts or a partnership with a larger energy player would also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share price is still below the 52‑week low, suggesting the crash is not yet fully exhausted. A sharp uptick in volume and a break above the short‑term moving average would signal the start of a mean‑reversion play.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company recovers to 50% of its pre‑crash price, the upside is roughly 100% relative to the current level, while the downside is capped at the current price. Historically, deep‑crash setups in this sector have returned 40–60% in the long run, and the lack of public sentiment means the market could surprise on a positive catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the regulatory review could uncover material compliance breaches, forcing a costly restructuring or even a delisting. If the company’s cash burn continues unchecked, it could run out of liquidity before the next funding round, tightening the price further.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100% of invested capital if the price stays flat or declines further. Potential gain: 100–150% if the price rebounds to 50–75% of pre‑crash levels. The asymmetry favors a long position because the upside far outweighs the limited downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 20‑day moving average with at least 20% higher volume than the 50‑day average. Size the position so that a 5% drop would wipe out 10% of the trade, keeping risk at 10% of the account. Place a stop 3% below the entry to protect against a brief reversal.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the compliance review is officially cleared or until the next earnings release confirms revenue growth. A 15% rise in institutional holdings or a new contract announcement would be the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the regulatory review flags a severe compliance breach or the company files for insolvency, exit immediately and cut losses.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CWR Ltd. sits on a deep crash with no market chatter; a positive regulatory outcome or earnings surprise could ignite a bounce that offers a 2:1 risk‑reward ratio.
